pattern: Only weight parity affects an odd total, so weight 2 acts like 0 and weight 1 like 1, reducing the problem to counting parity-odd binary strings of length equal to the deepest path.
hints: A weight of 2 never changes the parity of the path cost; only edges assigned weight 1 do. So the cost is odd exactly when an odd number of edges get weight 1.
The relevant path length is the maximum depth of the tree measured in edges from the root, node 1.
Among L independent edges, exactly half of all 2^L assignments flip to an odd count, giving 2^(L-1) modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: star, every leaf at depth one
edges listed child-first and out of order
deep chain not in root order

stress.description: random tree built by attaching each new node to an existing one, deepest path drives the answer
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    for v in range(2, n + 1):
        u = rng.randrange(1, v)
        edges.append([u, v])
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    return [edges]
